header.setup-draft.you.2: You must give a card to {player1} and {player2}
header.setup-draft.you.1: You must give {cards, plural, =1{one card} other{two cards}} to {player}.
header.setup-draft.players: Nobles must give {cards, plural, =1{one card} other{two cards}} to their neighbors
header.setup-draft.player.2: {player} must give a card to his neighbors
header.setup-draft.player.1: {player} must give {cards, plural, =1{one card} other{two cards}} to {neighbor}
header.setup-draft.player.you: {player} must give you {cards, plural, =1{one card} other{two cards}}
header.setup-build.you: Choose 1 card from your hand to build
header.setup-build.players: Nobles must choose 1 card from their hand to build
header.setup-build.player: {player} must choose 1 card from his hand to build
header.play-card.you: Discard a card for its effect or <pass>pass</pass>
header.play-card.player: {player} can discard a card for its effect
header.villager.you: The Villager: take the highest card from one of your Cities
header.villager.player: The Villager: {player} takes the highest card from one of their cities
header.jester.you: The Jester: you draw a card
header.assassin.you: The Assassin: discard the highest card from a City
header.assassin.player: The Assassin: {player} discards the highest card from a City
header.merchant.you: The Merchant: take 1 card from the Market
header.merchant.player: The Merchant: {player} takes 1 card from the Market
header.knight.you: The Knight: move the highest card from a City to yours
header.knight.player: The Knight: {player} moves the highest card from a City to theirs
header.astrologer.you: The Astrologer: build up to 2 of the 3 cards drawn or <pass>pass</pass>
header.astrologer.player: The Astrologer: {player} can build up to 2 of the 3 cards drawn
header.market-buy.you: Buy cards at the Market for {value} or <pass>pass</pass>
header.market-buy.player: {player} can buy cards from the market.
header.market-buy.cannot.you: You must <pass>add a card</pass> to the Market (purchase unavailable)
header.market-buy.cannot.player: {player} cannot buy, he must add a card to the Market
header.market-buy.validate: You can no longer buy, you must <validate>Validate</validate> or <undo>Cancel</undo>
header.market-draw.you: <draw>Add a card to the Market</draw> or <hero>Use your Hero</hero>
header.market-draw.player: {player} must add a card to the Market or use his Hero
header.market-draw.add.player: {player} adds a card to the market.
header.revolt.you.has-bought: Revolt! You can buy cards at the Market for {value} or <pass>pass</pass>
header.revolt.you: Revolt! You must buy cards at the Market for {value}
header.revolt.player: Revolt! {player} must buy cards at the Market
header.build.you: Choose cards from your hand to build or <pass>validate</pass>
header.build.players: Nobles must choose cards from their hand to build
header.build.player: {player} must choose cards from his hand to build
header.scoring.you: You win {vp, plural, one{# Victory Point} other{# Victory Points}} for the {city, select, 1{Purple} 2{Yellow} 3{Pink} 4{Red} 5{Blue} other{Green}} City
header.scoring.player: {player} wins {vp, plural, one{# Victory Point} other{# Victory Points}} for the {city, select, 1{Purple} 2{Yellow} 3{Pink} 4{Red} 5{Blue} other{Green}} City

history.catchup: Nobles behind in the score draw cards at the end of the season.
history.season-end: The {season, select, 1{Spring} 2{Summer} 3{Autumn} other{Winter}} season has come to an end.
history.end-game.hero.you: Your People's Hero earns you 2 points.
history.end-game.hero.player: {player} earns 2 points for his People's Hero.
description.card.0: <0>Discard 1 face up card from the Market.</0> There is only one 0 card per color. The 0 cards do not allow control over a majority.
description.card.1: <0>Take the highest card from 1 of your Cities into your hand.</0> You cannot retrieve a covered card.
description.card.2: <0>Draw 1 card</0> and add it to your hand.
description.card.3: <0>Discard the highest card from 1 of your Cities or an opponent’s City.</0> You cannot discard a covered card.
description.card.4: <0>Take 1 face up card from the Market</0> without paying its cost and add it to your hand.
description.card.5: <0>Move the highest card from any City to 1 of yours.</0> You cannot choose a covered card or one that you cannot build. With the Knight, you can move a Wild card (either yours or an opponent’s) and build it alone (without needing another card) in 1 of your Cities.
description.card.6: <0>Draw 3 cards. Build 0, 1, or 2 of them following the construction rules and discard the others.</0> The effects of these cards are not triggered.
description.card.white: White cards can be built in any City, as long as they are associated with a card of the color of the City in which you are building them.
description.card.draw-pile: There are {cards} {cards, plural, =1{card} other{cards}} left in the draw pile.
description.hero.title: People’s Hero or Heroine
description.hero.recto: The People's Hero card is placed face up in the event of a Revolt.
description.hero.verso: If your People’s Hero or Heroine is face up, when you ADD ONE CARD TO THE MARKET, you can draw 2 cards (instead of 1) and choose which one to add to the Market
description.hero.vp: If your Hero or Heroine is still face up at the end of the game, you will earn 2 Victory Points.
description.token.title: {type, select, 8{Noble} other{Market}} token of {player}
description.token.points: {player}'s score is {points, plural, one{# point} other{# points}}.
description.half-card.2: If you haven't bought this season yet, not triggering a revolt earns you 1 victory point. Triggering one gives 1 victory point to the other Nobles.
description.half-card.3: If you haven't bought this season yet, not triggering a revolt earns you 2 victory points. Triggering one gives 2 victory points to other Nobles.
description.half-card.4: If you haven't bought this season yet, not triggering a revolt earns you 3 victory points. Triggering one gives 3 victory points to other Nobles.
description.city-board.1: <0>Purple City</0>: earn 1 Victory Point for each visible Crown in this City. (Only purple cards have crowns.)
description.city-board.2: <0>Yellow City</0>: earn 1 Victory Point for each card in your City that has the most cards.
description.city-board.3: <0>Pink City</0>: divide the total Influence of this City by 3. Earn the resulting amount in Victory Points, rounded down (minimum 1 Victory Point).
description.city-board.4: <0>Red City</0>: earn 1 Victory Point for each of your Cities that has at least 2 cards, including the red City. If none of your Cities have 2 or more cards, you earn 1 Victory Point.
description.city-board.5: <0>Blue City</0>: earn 1 Victory Point for each of your Cities that has a Villager (1) and/or a Jester (2). A City with both 1 Villager and 1 Jester still only gives you 1 Victory Point. If none of your Cities have a Villager or Jester, you earn 1 Victory Point.
description.city-board.6: <0>Green City</0>: earn 1 Victory Point for each set of 3 cards across all your Cities (minimum 1 Victory Point).
description.city-board.title: {player}'s board
tuto.goal: You play the role of a Nobleman seeking to conquer the throne. Build the most influential cities to win!
tuto.city-board: Here's your <bold>Cities board</bold>. This is where you'll add cards to increase your influence in the kingdom's 6 Cities.
tuto.subject-cards: Here are the <bold>Subject cards</bold>. Each subject belongs to a City, except those with a white background, which can go to any City.
tuto.give-cards:
At the start of the game, you must give 1 card to your left and right neighbors.
With 2 Nobles, you give 2 cards to your opponent.
Give him these 2 cards.
tuto.setup-build.players:
Your opponent has given you 2 cards.
From the 3 cards in your hand, choose 1 to add to your Cities.
tuto.setup-build.build: Add this card by dragging it to the highlighted space next to your Cities.
tuto.setup-build.first-player: You've played the card with the lowest influence (value), now it's your turn to start.
tuto.seasons:
The game is played in <bold>4 Seasons</bold> (3 Seasons with 4 Nobles).
At the end of each Season, each of the 6 Cities awards <bold>Victory Points</bold> to the Noble with the most influence in that City.
The Noble with the most Victory Points at the end of the game wins.
tuto.play-card.details:
On your turn, you can discard a card <bold>from your hand</bold> or <bold>the highest from one of your Cities</bold> to trigger its effect.
Cards range from 0 to 6, so there are 7 different effects (cards of the same value have the same effect). <bold>At any time, click on a card to read its effect.</bold>
Click <bold>Pass</bold> in the header for this round.
tuto.buy-cards.details: Then you can <bold>buy cards from the Market</bold>.
tuto.buy-cards.purchasing-power:
You can only buy cards <bold>once per Season</bold>.
Your purchasing capacity corresponds to the number of cards in the Market and Reserve (face-down cards). The cost of the cards corresponds to their value.
<italic>You can decide to buy only if the first row of the Market contains at least 4 cards</italic>.
tuto.buy-cards.pass:
Here, you could buy cards worth a total of 8, but it might be worth waiting a while.
Click <bold>Pass</bold> in the header for this round.
tuto.add-card: When you don't buy cards, <bold>you must add a card</bold> to the Market at the end of your turn.
tuto.add-card.risk:
<bold>Beware</bold>: if the card you draw is the 3rd card of its suit in the Market, you cause a <bold>Revolt</bold>!
Here, it's the 2nd Yellow card, so all's well.
tuto.opponent.add-card: Your opponent has not discarded a card to trigger an effect. He hasn't bought a card either, so he adds a card to the Market.
tuto.opponent.revolt: Bad luck, he's triggered a Revolt by drawing a 3rd Yellow card!
tuto.opponent.discard-cards: The 2 yellow cards already in the Market are discarded.
tuto.opponent.give-points-revolt: As he has triggered a Revolt by adding a card to the 3rd row, you earn 1 Victory Point.
tuto.score-board: Victory points are counted on this score track.
tuto.opponent.buy-cards:
The Noble who triggers a Revolt then <bold>has to buy</bold> cards at the Market.
Your opponent has a purchasing capacity of 8 (there are 8 cards in the Market).
tuto.play-jester:
Now it's your turn.
This turn, discard the Jester (2) from your hand to play its effect: draw the first card from the deck. Slide your card onto the discard pile.
tuto.you.cannot-buy: Here, you can't buy because the second line of the market is not filled. <bold>Add a card to the Market.</bold>
tuto.opponent.still-play:
Although your opponent has bought at the Market, he continues to play: on his turn, he can discard a card to trigger its effect.
He also adds a card to the Market, but cannot buy any more cards.
If he triggers a Revolt, <bold>he can play the effect</bold> of the smaller of the 2 discarded cards for free.
tuto.play-card.pass: It's up to you. Click <bold>Pass</bold> for a start.
tuto.buy-cards.buy: You can buy cards at the Market. This time, buy the highlighted cards by dragging them to your hand.
tuto.build.start:
Once all Nobles have bought at the Market, the season ends.
The <bold>City Construction</bold> phase begins.
tuto.build.details:
At this stage, each Noble secretly selects the cards from his hand that he wishes to build.
You must always build your Cities <bold>in ascending order</bold>.
White cards are wildcards and can go into any City, but they can't be built alone: you must play them with another card of the color of the City you're adding it to.
tuto.build.play: Build all the cards in your hand by sliding them into their respective Cities. Play your white card in the Pink City. To do this, you'll need to build it with your Pink card.
tuto.build.confirm: Click <bold>Validate</bold> in the header to complete your build step.
tuto.build.reveal: Once all the Nobles have finished building their cities, the built cards are revealed.
tuto.majorities.start:
Then, each City awards Victory Points to the Noble who has the greatest influence there.
<italic>In the event of a tie, all Nobles with the most influence earn Victory Points.</italic>
tuto.majorities.details:
Each City has its own rules for awarding Victory Points.
<bold>Click on a City board to find out the rules for each City.</bold>
tuto.majorities.purple: You have more influence than your opponent in the <bold>Purple City</bold>. You therefore gain <bold>1 point per visible crown</bold> i.e.: 1 point.
tuto.majorities.yellow: Your opponent wins the majority in the <bold>Yellow City</bold>. He gains <bold>1 point per card in his highest City</bold> i.e.: 1 point.
tuto.majorities.pink: You have more influence than your opponent in the <bold>Pink City</bold>. You <bold>divide your influence in this City by 3</bold> (rounded down). Here you gain 3 points.
tuto.majorities.red: Even if your opponent has a card in the <bold>Red City</bold>, it has a value of 0. So no one wins the majority.
tuto.majorities.blue: Your opponent wins the majority in the <bold>Blue City</bold>. He gains <bold>1 point per city where there is at least one Villager (1) or Jester (2)</bold> card. Here, he has none, however, the minimum number of points won is 1.
tuto.majorities.green: Your opponent wins the majority in the <bold>Green City</bold>. He gains <bold>1 point for every 3 cards in his kingdom</bold> (rounded down). He built 5 cards, so he earns 1 point.
tuto.history: The 🗨 button at top left gives access to the game history. Click on it for details of the Victory Points awarded to each Noble for each City.
tuto.catchup: If a Noble falls behind on the Victory Point track, he draws 1 card for each Bonus square (in black) between him and the person in the lead.
tuto.hero:
When your opponent triggered a Revolt, in compensation he was able to turn over his <bold>People's Hero card</bold>.
He can use this by turning it face-down, to draw 2 cards during the Market addition phase. He can then choose which one he adds: this makes it possible to avoid or deliberately provoke a Revolt.
If this card is face-up at the end of the game, it earns 2 Victory Points.
tuto.end:
The game continues until the end of the Seasons.
Good luck!
